# Discharge Summary

## Basic Information
- **Name**: John Smith
- **Gender**: Male
- **Age**: 65 years
- **Hospitalization Number**: 20260201001
- **Admission Date**: February 1, 2026
- **Discharge Date**: February 10, 2026
- **Admission Diagnosis**: 1. Coronary atherosclerotic heart disease 2. Unstable angina pectoris 3. Hypertension grade 3 (very high risk)
- **Discharge Diagnosis**: 1. Coronary atherosclerotic heart disease 2. Unstable angina pectoris 3. Hypertension grade 3 (very high risk) 4. Type 2 diabetes mellitus

## Hospitalization Course

### February 1, 2026
- **Admission Condition**: The patient was admitted due to "recurrent chest pain for 2 weeks, aggravated for 1 day". On admission, the chest pain was oppressive, located in the precordial area, about the size of a palm, radiating to the left shoulder, lasting about 15 minutes, relieved after rest.
- **Physical Examination**: Temperature 36.5℃, pulse 72 beats/min, respiration 18 breaths/min, blood pressure 160/95mmHg. No cardiac enlargement, heart rate 72 beats/min, regular rhythm, no murmurs in all valve auscultation areas.
- **Auxiliary Examination**: Electrocardiogram: Sinus rhythm, ST segment depression 0.1-0.2mV in V1-V4 leads. Blood routine, liver and kidney function, electrolytes were normal.

### February 2, 2026
- **Examination**: Myocardial enzymes: Troponin I 0.05ng/ml (normal), CK-MB 15U/L (normal). Echocardiogram: Left ventricular ejection fraction 60%, normal chamber sizes, no obvious abnormal wall motion.
- **Treatment**: Aspirin 100mg qd, Clopidogrel 75mg qd, Atorvastatin 20mg qn, Metoprolol 25mg bid, Valsartan 80mg qd, Isosorbide mononitrate 20mg bid.

### February 3, 2026
- **Examination**: Coronary angiography: No significant stenosis in left main coronary artery, 80% stenosis in proximal left anterior descending artery, 50% stenosis in mid circumflex artery, 40% stenosis in mid right coronary artery.
- **Diagnosis**: Coronary atherosclerotic heart disease, unstable angina pectoris, three-vessel disease.

### February 4, 2026
- **Operation**: Coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG)
  - **Surgeons**: Dr. Wang (chief surgeon), Dr. Li (assistant)
  - **Operation Time**: 08:30-14:30
  - **Operation Method**: Off-pump coronary artery bypass grafting
  - **Grafts**: Left internal mammary artery → left anterior descending artery, great saphenous vein → circumflex artery, great saphenous vein → right coronary artery
  - **Intraoperative Condition**: The operation was successful, blood loss about 300ml, no blood transfusion.

### February 5, 2026
- **Postoperative Condition**: The patient was transferred to intensive care unit, with mechanical ventilation, vital signs stable.
- **Treatment**: Dopamine 3μg/kg/min intravenous pump, nitroglycerin 5μg/min intravenous pump, cefuroxime 1.5g q8h intravenous drip, omeprazole 40mg q12h intravenous injection.

### February 6, 2026
- **Postoperative Condition**: The patient was extubated, changed to nasal cannula oxygen, oxygen saturation 98%.
- **Treatment**: Discontinued dopamine, continued nitroglycerin intravenous pump, adjusted to 3μg/min.

### February 7, 2026
- **Postoperative Condition**: The patient was transferred to general ward, vital signs stable, no chest pain, dyspnea or other discomfort.
- **Examination**: Blood routine: White blood cell count 8.5×10⁹/L, hemoglobin 120g/L, platelet count 180×10⁹/L. Liver and kidney function, electrolytes were normal.
- **Treatment**: Discontinued nitroglycerin intravenous pump, changed to isosorbide mononitrate 20mg bid oral.

### February 8, 2026
- **Examination**: Blood glucose: Fasting blood glucose 7.8mmol/L, 2-hour postprandial blood glucose 11.2mmol/L.
- **Diagnosis**: Type 2 diabetes mellitus.
- **Treatment**: Added metformin 500mg bid oral.

### February 9, 2026
- **Examination**: Electrocardiogram: Sinus rhythm, ST segment in V1-V4 leads improved compared to previous.
- **Treatment**: Adjusted metoprolol dose to 50mg bid oral.

### February 10, 2026
- **Discharge Assessment**: The patient's general condition was good, no chest pain, dyspnea or other discomfort, vital signs stable. Wound healing was good, no redness, exudation.
- **Discharge Instructions**: See discharge instructions section.

## Discharge Instructions

### Medication Treatment
- **Antiplatelet drugs**: Aspirin 100mg qd, lifetime use; Clopidogrel 75mg qd, use for 12 months
- **Lipid-lowering drugs**: Atorvastatin 20mg qn, lifetime use
- **β-blockers**: Metoprolol 50mg bid, adjust dose according to heart rate
- **ACEI/ARB**: Valsartan 80mg qd, lifetime use
- **Nitrates**: Isosorbide mononitrate 20mg bid, can gradually reduce dose after symptom relief
- **Antidiabetic drugs**: Metformin 500mg bid, monitor blood glucose
- **Antibiotics**: Cefuroxime axetil 250mg bid, use for 3 days

### Diet and Activity
- **Diet**: Low-salt, low-fat, diabetic diet, control total calories, daily salt intake <5g
- **Activity**: Avoid strenuous exercise within 1 month after surgery, can perform mild activities such as walking; gradually resume normal activities after 3 months
- **Weight**: Control weight, target BMI <24kg/m²

### Follow-up Plan
- **1 week after discharge**: Cardiology outpatient follow-up, recheck blood routine, liver and kidney function, electrolytes
- **2 weeks after discharge**: Suture removal, thoracic surgery outpatient follow-up
- **1 month after discharge**: Cardiology outpatient follow-up, recheck electrocardiogram, echocardiogram
- **3 months after discharge**: Cardiology outpatient follow-up, recheck coronary CTA
- **6 months after discharge**: Cardiology outpatient follow-up, comprehensive assessment of cardiac function

### Notes
- Keep the wound clean and dry, avoid infection
- Take medication on time, do not adjust dosage without authorization
- Regularly monitor blood pressure and blood glucose
- Seek medical attention promptly if chest pain, dyspnea, fever or other discomfort occurs
- Quit smoking and limit alcohol, maintain good living habits
